issues: normalization runtime CMake is not the full nanobind build surface
the existing workspace already requires device/layernorm_types.hpp in addition to layernorm_common.hpp
the baseline needs an explicit row-local stats strategy that does not preload Wt tiles into L1

exact_signature: std::tuple<uint32_t, CoreRangeSet, CoreRangeSet, CoreRangeSet, uint32_t, uint32_t> split_work_to_cores(CoreCoord grid_size, uint32_t units_to_divide, bool row_wise = false);

cb_requirements.notes: row units can be distributed without splitting a logical row across cores

usage_pattern: append all scalar CT args first, then append stable accessor slots for input/residual/weight/bias/output

exact_signature: template <PoolType reduce_type, ReduceDim reduce_dim, ReduceInputPolicy input_policy = ReduceInputPolicy::WaitAndPopPerTile, ReduceDataFormatReconfigMode reconfig_mode = ReduceDataFormatReconfigMode::INPUT_AND_OUTPUT, typename AccumulateT = NoAccumulation, typename PostReduceOp = NoOp> ALWI void reduce(uint32_t input_cb, uint32_t scaler_cb, uint32_t output_cb, ReduceInputBlockShape input_block_shape, ReduceInputMemoryLayout input_memory_layout = ReduceInputMemoryLayout::contiguous(), AccumulateT accumulate = AccumulateT{}, PostReduceOp post_reduce_op = PostReduceOp{});

cb_requirements.accumulation: output CB may also serve as rolling accumulator via Accumulate::at(cb, iteration)

exact_signature: template <BinaryOpType op_type, BroadcastDim bcast_dim = BroadcastDim::NONE, BinaryInputPolicy input_a_policy = BinaryInputPolicy::WaitAndPopPerTile, BinaryInputPolicy input_b_policy = input_a_policy, BinaryOutputPolicy output_policy = BinaryOutputPolicy::PerTile, BinaryDataFormatReconfig reconfig = BinaryDataFormatReconfig::INPUT_AND_OUTPUT, bool init = true, typename PostOp = NoOp, typename AccumT = NoAccumulation> ALWI void binary_op(uint32_t icb_a, uint32_t icb_b, uint32_t ocb, BinaryInputBlockShape shape, PostOp post_op = {}, AccumT accum = {});

cb_requirements.init: call binary_op_init_common(...) once before helper usage
cb_requirements.broadcast: COL for row stats, ROW for affine, NONE for mask

exact_signature: template <bool half_tile = false> FORCE_INLINE void generate_reduce_scaler(const uint32_t cb_id, const uint32_t scaler);
FORCE_INLINE void fill_cb_with_value(uint32_t cb_id, uint32_t value, int32_t num_of_elems = 1024);

cb_requirements.rationale: seed epsilon scalar tile in intermed precision

exact_signature: template <typename T = uint16_t> FORCE_INLINE void generate_mask_w(uint32_t cb_mask, uint32_t mask_w);

cb_requirements.format: same data format as masked operand
cb_requirements.rationale: reuse one tail-width mask tile for stats and final output masking

exact_signature: ALWI void pack_tile_with_dt(uint32_t ifrom_dst, uint32_t icb); ALWI void copy_tile_init_with_dt(uint32_t icb, uint32_t transpose = 0);

cb_requirements.rationale: used for copy fallbacks when optional paths are disabled

exact_signature: template <typename device_operation_t> auto compute_program_hash(const typename device_operation_t::operation_attributes_t& operation_attributes, const typename device_operation_t::tensor_args_t& tensor_args);
usage_pattern: omit custom hash to get default attrs+tensors hashing, or provide a custom layernorm hash with explicit shape-sensitive fields

actual: nanobind translation units are enumerated separately in ttnn/CMakeLists.txt:367-368 while normalization/CMakeLists.txt:14-35 only covers runtime sources and kernel packaging
resolution: add layernorm runtime/device .cpp files to normalization/CMakeLists.txt, add layernorm_nanobind.cpp to ttnn/CMakeLists.txt, and register bind_layernorm_operation(...) from normalization_nanobind.cpp

architect_said: layernorm_common.hpp carries the shared primitive/config surface
actual: dit_rms_norm_unary_fused.hpp:11-29 already includes ttnn/operations/normalization/layernorm/device/layernorm_types.hpp and dit_rms_norm_unary_fused.cpp:6-7 also includes layernorm_common.hpp
resolution: create both device/layernorm_types.hpp and device/layernorm_common.hpp; keep types/config enums in layernorm_types.hpp and helper declarations/aliases in layernorm_common.hpp

actual: Phase 2b row ownership never splits a logical row across cores, so there is no cross-core partial merge to perform; direct mean and mean(x^2) accumulation via reduce<..., Accumulate> keeps CBs O(1)
resolution: engineer the baseline around row-local SUM reductions scaled by 1/logical_W, derive var = mean(x^2) - mean^2, and reserve Welford/distributed combine for later sharded/distributed factories

architect_said: register stages with the standard orchestrator flow
actual: tdd_orchestrator.py init derives op_name from op_path.name (layernorm), but the public op symbol for this workflow is layer_norm
resolution: keep the orchestrator state schema but set .tdd_state.json["op_name"] = "layer_norm" so future test generation calls the correct public wrapper

architect_said: verify shape-sensitive cache identity before finalizing the hash
actual: batch_norm_device_operation.cpp:121-158 hashes attrs + dtype/memory metadata but not explicit padded shapes, and the generic device_operation fallback in device_operation.hpp:56-67 is bypassed once a custom hash is defined
resolution: define an explicit LayerNormOperation custom hash that includes input padded shape and optional tensor presence + padded-shape metadata because Wt/num_units directly change the program

why: REDUCE_ROW in streaming mode waits/pops one tile at a time and only requires >=1 page in the input CB, while Bulk/Upfront modes need row-sized availability and would scale with Wt; the row-local baseline therefore keeps stats buffers O(1) and rereads input for pass1 instead of preloading whole rows

name: input_pass0
rationale: single-tile streaming reader/compute boundary; 2 pages leave room for DMA/compute overlap without changing helper wait counts

name: residual_pass0
rationale: mirrors input_pass0 for optional pre-add on the stats pass

name: input_pass1
rationale: separate reread stream for normalization pass keeps pass0 stats consumption independent

name: residual_pass1
rationale: optional residual reread on the normalize pass

name: weight_pass1
rationale: row-broadcast affine tiles stream once per width tile during pass1

name: bias_pass1
rationale: same streaming pattern as weight_pass1

name: reduce_scaler_inv_width
rationale: generate once and reuse for every row reduction

name: epsilon_scalar
rationale: single scalar tile broadcast into the final rsqrt pre-op

name: mask_w
rationale: one persistent tail mask tile is sufficient because only the final width tile of each row needs masking

name: output
rationale: streamed compute/writer boundary; 2 pages are enough for single-tile output cadence

name: mean_accum
rationale: rolling row accumulator/output tile; same CB reused across Wt iterations via Accumulate::at(cb_mean, tile_idx)

name: sqmean_accum
rationale: rolling row accumulator for mean(x^2)

name: invstd
rationale: one row-local COL-broadcast tile persists across the normalization pass

name: scratch0
rationale: tile-local scratch for masked source / centered tile / post-bias tile

name: scratch1
rationale: tile-local scratch for mean^2 / variance / affine staging

name: num_rows_per_core
source: num_units_per_core_group_{1,2} from split_work_to_cores(...) for the owning core

name: tile_offset
source: running base tile offset; increment by num_rows_per_core * Wt per core

name: inv_width_bf16_packed
source: packed BF16 pair encoding 1.0f / input.logical_shape()[-1] for generate_reduce_scaler(...)

name: is_rmsnorm
source: compile-time flag derived from operation_attributes.norm_type == LayerNormType::RMSNORM

name: num_rows_per_core
source: same row count passed to the reader for row-local pass0/pass1 synchronization

name: Wt
source: row width in tiles for both stats and normalize loops

name: logical_W
source: input.logical_shape()[-1] to derive tail masking and 1/logical_W scaling

name: tile_offset
source: same per-core base tile index used by the reader so output tiles are written in input traversal order

name: data_pipeline
rationale: establish reader/compute/writer plumbing, row scheduling, and final-width masking before any stats math

name: mean_reduce
rationale: verify pass0 masked row accumulation and temporary reduced-shape output before variance/rsqrt logic is added

name: invstd_reduce
rationale: lock the row-stat finalization path (`mean(x^2) - mean^2`, epsilon add, rsqrt) while output is still one-tile wide
reference_body: mean = input_tensor.float().mean(dim=-1, keepdim=True); var = ((input_tensor.float() - mean) ** 2).mean(dim=-1, keepdim=True); return torch.rsqrt(var + 1e-5)

name: normalize
rationale: promote the op back to full-shape output and verify mean subtraction plus invstd broadcast without optional tensors

name: residual_affine
rationale: add the remaining reader streams and row-broadcast affine paths after the base normalization math is stable
reference_body: x = input_tensor.float() + residual_tensor.float(); return torch.nn.functional.layer_norm(x, (x.shape[-1],), weight=weight_tensor.float().reshape(-1), bias=bias_tensor.float().reshape(-1), eps=1e-5)

name: acceptance
rationale: final cross-product over residual/weight/bias/tail-width/epsilon on the public wrapper; no new kernel code should be introduced here
reference_body: x = input_tensor.float(); x = x if residual_tensor is None else x + residual_tensor.float(); w = None if weight_tensor is None else weight_tensor.float().reshape(-1); b = None if bias_tensor is None else bias_tensor.float().reshape(-1); return torch.nn.functional.layer_norm(x, (x.shape[-1],), weight=w, bias=b, eps=epsilon)
